     </Exec>

                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />

     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Analysis

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild cannot skip it, so it runs every build.
- **Volatile content** — uses `DateTime.Now`, guaranteeing the output file changes every time, which can invalidate downstream targets.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — always re-executes.
- **Unconditional overwrite** — writes `git-hash.g.cs` every build even when the hash hasn't changed. Because the file timestamp updates, Roslyn sees it as modified and recompiles.
- **Missing `<FileWrites>`** — the generated file won't be cleaned by `dotnet clean`.

### Impact

Even with zero source changes, both targets run and produce new file timestamps, forcing a full recompile on every build (no-op builds are impossible).

### Fixes

| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` or remove `DateTime.Now` (volatile). If a timestamp is truly needed, gate writes behind a content-comparison check. |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="$(ProjectDir).git/HEAD;$(ProjectDir).git/refs/heads/**"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"` so the target skips when HEAD hasn't changed. Register the output in `<FileWrites>`. |

Would you like me to apply the fixes to the `.csproj`?
data.reasoningText:
Now I can analyze the incremental build issues in this project. Let me identify the problems:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp` target** (line 6-11):
   - No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes on the target
   - Uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` which is a volatile value — different on every build
   - Writes to `build-timestamp.txt` every build, changing the file content each time
   - This forces the target to run every time AND changes the output file, which could trigger downstream targets

2. **`EmbedGitHash` target** (line 13-23):
   - Also missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so it runs unconditionally
   - Executes git commands on every build to fetch the commit hash, then writes the generated source file with `Overwrite="true"` regardless of whether the content actually changed
   - Since this file is part of the `<Compile>` items, even timestamp-only changes can force unnecessary recompilation
   - The generated file isn't tracked in `<FileWrites>`, so the Clean target won't properly remove it
